evals.prompt: I built a customer support chatbot using Claude and deployed it to staging at https://staging.myapp.com/api/chat. It accepts JSON with a 'message' field and uses Bearer token auth. I want to pen test it before we go live next week. Can you walk me through how to use this skill to run the full test suite?
evals.expected_output: A clear, actionable walkthrough: install deps, set env vars, run infra tests, run AI behavioral tests, generate report. Should show the exact commands to run. Tone should be practical, not overwhelming.
assertions: Response includes the three main script commands (run_infra_tests.py, run_ai_tests.py, generate_report.py)
Response shows how to set PENTEST_ENDPOINT, PENTEST_TOKEN environment variables
Response mentions installing requests and rich dependencies
Response explains the difference between infra tests (automated) and AI behavioral tests (interactive or auto-grade)
Response is well-organized and easy to follow

evals.prompt:
I ran the pen test on my AI chat API and got these results:
- Categories 1-5: all passed
- Category 6 (Output Exploitation): Test 6.2 failed — the model explained its guardrail mechanism in detail
- G3 (Rate Limiting): FAIL — we have no cooldown between messages
- G6 (HTML Injection): FAIL — script tags aren't stripped

Can you summarize what this means and give me a prioritized remediation plan?
evals.expected_output: A clear, prioritized remediation plan. G6 (XSS/injection) should be highest priority as it's a server-side security issue. G3 (rate limiting) is medium. Category 6 fix requires a system prompt change. Should be actionable and specific.
assertions: Response identifies G6 (HTML injection) as the highest priority fix
Response gives specific fix for G3 (rate limiting) — per-user cooldown
Response gives specific fix for G6 — strip HTML/script tags server-side
Response gives specific fix for 6.2 — system prompt instruction about not explaining guardrail mechanisms
Remediation guidance matches what's in the framework

evals.prompt: I want to run this pen test in our CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions so it runs automatically before every production deployment. We use a staging environment. How do I set this up?
evals.expected_output: Practical CI/CD setup guidance: store secrets in GitHub Secrets, reference them in workflow, use --auto-grade flag, check exit code for pass/fail gate. Should include a GitHub Actions YAML snippet.
assertions: Response mentions storing credentials as GitHub Secrets (not hardcoded)
Response mentions the --auto-grade flag for unattended runs
Response includes a GitHub Actions YAML example or snippet
Response mentions using exit code to gate the deployment
Response mentions ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is needed for auto-grade mode
